Title: Structural investigation of heteroyohimbine alkaloid synthesis reveals active site elements that control stereoselectivity

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized alkaloid scaffold with pharmacological relevance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies strictosidine aglycone precursor for heteroyohimbine formation and physically associates with THAS1/2/HYS in the nucleus
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SGD deglycosylates strictosidine to generate the reactive strictosidine aglycone precursor for heteroyohimbine biosynthesis. 2. SGD is nuclear-localized and interacts with THAS1, THAS2 and HYS. 3. These interactions support channeling or recruitment at this branch point in monoterpene indole alkaloid metabolism.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Nuclear punctate localization in C. roseus cells
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Produces a reactive and potentially toxic aglycone intermediate
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important partner enzyme for pathway reconstruction and potential metabolon engineering with HYS/THAS enzymes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to the pathway, SGD mainly provides precursor rather than determining product stereochemistry in this study

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Used as a specificity control showing no interaction with SGD in the nucleus
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 16OMT did not interact with SGD in BiFC assays. 2. It served as a negative control supporting specificity of THAS/SGD and HYS/SGD interactions.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a core metabolism gene for heteroyohimbine causality; included only as interaction control

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Sunstorm Apricot V1.0 transcript source; NA
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch point, downstream of strictosidine glucosidase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: NADPH-dependent reduction of strictosidine aglycone/cathenamine to heteroyohimbine alkaloids, producing mainly tetrahydroalstonine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized defense-associated alkaloid chemistry; heteroyohimbine scaffold with pharmacological relevance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes tetrahydroalstonine formation and contributes to heteroyohimbine accumulation in planta; silencing decreased ajmalicine and tetrahydroalstonine in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THAS1 reduces strictosidine aglycone to predominantly tetrahydroalstonine with minor mayumbine. 2. Crystal structure and docking identified active-site features for stereoselective reduction. 3. Deuterium-labeling showed hydride transfer to the pro-R position at C21. 4. THAS1 localizes preferentially to the nucleus and interacts with SGD. 5. VIGS of THAS1 significantly reduced leaf ajmalicine and tetrahydroalstonine levels.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-point enzyme; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression and enzyme assay; LC-MS product profiling; X-ray crystallography; docking; deuterium-labeling NMR; site-directed mutagenesis; subcellular localization; BiFC; VIGS
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Selected from transcriptome with non-negligible expression in young and mature leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves; nuclear-localized in C. roseus cells
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Assay pH 5-8 tested; THAS1 product profile was largely pH-insensitive
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Catalytic redundancy complicates physiological assignment; low endogenous heteroyohimbine abundance and high leaf-to-leaf variability
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for metabolic engineering of heteroyohimbine scaffolds and stereochemical control in monoterpene indole alkaloid production
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from C. roseus Sunstorm Apricot V1.0; untargeted metabolomics in VIGS leaves (no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: THAS1 holo and apo structures solved; mutations such as E59A and Y56S altered activity/promiscuity more than product identity

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Sunstorm Apricot V1.0 transcript source; NA
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch point, downstream of strictosidine glucosidase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: NADPH-dependent reduction of strictosidine aglycone to mainly tetrahydroalstonine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized alkaloid with pharmacological scaffold relevance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces tetrahydroalstonine in vitro but silencing showed no clear decrease in leaf heteroyohimbines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THAS2 was identified as an active MDR homolog reducing strictosidine aglycone. 2. It produces ~85% tetrahydroalstonine with minor mayumbine. 3. Crystal structure revealed an alternative active-site configuration compared with THAS1. 4. THAS2 localizes to both cytosol and nucleus and interacts with SGD in the nucleus. 5. VIGS did not provide evidence for reduced ajmalicine or tetrahydroalstonine.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-point enzyme candidate; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression and enzyme assay; LC-MS; X-ray crystallography; subcellular localization; BiFC; VIGS
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Selected from transcriptome with leaf expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Nucleocytosolic in transiently transformed C. roseus cells
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Physiological contribution unclear due to redundancy and low endogenous metabolite levels
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative tetrahydroalstonine-forming catalyst for pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from C. roseus Sunstorm Apricot V1.0
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: kcat(observed) much lower than THAS1 under assay conditions

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Sunstorm Apricot V1.0 transcript source; NA
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch point, downstream of strictosidine glucosidase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: NADPH-dependent reduction of strictosidine aglycone to mainly tetrahydroalstonine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized alkaloid scaffold with pharmacological relevance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces tetrahydroalstonine in vitro; silencing showed no clear in planta decrease in heteroyohimbines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THAS3 was discovered by screening MDR homologs from the C. roseus transcriptome. 2. It converted strictosidine aglycone to ~85% tetrahydroalstonine with minor mayumbine. 3. kcat(observed) was measurable but lower than THAS1. 4. VIGS did not reveal a significant decrease in ajmalicine or tetrahydroalstonine.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-point enzyme candidate; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression and enzyme assay; LC-MS; VIGS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Additional reductase for constructing heteroyohimbine-producing systems
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from C. roseus Sunstorm Apricot V1.0
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No crystal structure or localization reported

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Sunstorm Apricot V1.0 transcript source; NA
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch point, downstream of strictosidine glucosidase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: NADPH-dependent reduction of strictosidine aglycone to mainly tetrahydroalstonine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized alkaloid scaffold with pharmacological relevance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces tetrahydroalstonine in vitro; in planta role unresolved because THAS4 and HYS were co-silenced
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THAS4 was identified as an active heteroyohimbine synthase producing mostly tetrahydroalstonine. 2. It belongs to the MDR family but differs at some active-site residues from THAS1/2. 3. Its in planta role could not be individually resolved because THAS4 and HYS were too similar for separate VIGS.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-point enzyme candidate; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression and enzyme assay; LC-MS; combined VIGS with HYS
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High sequence identity with HYS (~91%) prevented separate gene silencing
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential reductase for heteroyohimbine pathway engineering, though difficult to genetically separate from HYS in this study
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from C. roseus Sunstorm Apricot V1.0
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Active-site cofactor anchoring residue inferred as Tyr rather than Glu/Asp

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Sunstorm Apricot V1.0 transcript source; NA
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch point, downstream of strictosidine glucosidase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: NADPH-dependent reduction of strictosidine aglycone to a stereochemical mixture of heteroyohimbine diastereomers, prominently ajmalicine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized alkaloid scaffold with pharmacological activities; ajmalicine is an α1-adrenergic receptor antagonist
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ajmalicine formation and broader heteroyohimbine stereochemical diversity; silencing significantly decreased ajmalicine in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HYS was identified as a distinct MDR homolog producing a mixture of ajmalicine, tetrahydroalstonine and mayumbine. 2. Structural analysis linked its extended loop 2 and His127 to altered stereoselectivity. 3. Loop 2 swap or H127A/H127N mutations converted HYS to a THAS-like product profile. 4. HYS localizes preferentially to the nucleus and interacts with SGD. 5. VIGS of the HYS/THAS4 fragment reduced ajmalicine in leaves, supporting an in planta role in ajmalicine production.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-switch enzyme controlling heteroyohimbine stereochemical outcome
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression and enzyme assay; LC-MS product profiling; X-ray crystallography; mutagenesis; deuterium-labeling NMR; pH profiling; subcellular localization; BiFC; VIGS
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression not higher than other synthases based on profile data; transcript detected in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially nuclear in transiently transformed C. roseus cells
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Lower pH increased ajmalicine relative to tetrahydroalstonine in HYS assays
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Separate in planta assignment is confounded by co-silencing with THAS4 and endogenous metabolite variability
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High value for engineering ajmalicine-biased or stereodiverse heteroyohimbine production; loop 2/His127 provides an engineering handle for stereochemical control
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from C. roseus Sunstorm Apricot V1.0; untargeted metabolomics in VIGS leaves (no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: His127 is required for ajmalicine-associated R-C20 stereochemistry; apo crystal structure solved
